instruction: Add a new section titled 'Background' with one sentence of body text, placed before \end{document} in main.tex.
Add the amsmath package to the preamble and insert a displayed equation $E=mc^2$ in the body.
There is a \cite{smith2020} with no matching bibliography entry. Add a references.bib with a matching entry and wire up \bibliography and \bibliographystyle in main.tex so it compiles.
Create a new file methods.tex containing a 'Methods' section, and \input it from main.tex before \end{document}.
Add an itemize list with three items describing contributions, inside the existing section.
Add a title, author, and \maketitle to the document.
Add an abstract environment with one sentence before the first section.
Insert a numbered equation using the equation environment (load amsmath) for the quadratic formula.
Add a second section 'Results' after the existing one with a short sentence.
Add a tabular with two columns and two rows summarizing a comparison.
Add the hyperref package and a \url to https://example.com in the body.
Convert the inline math x^2 + y^2 in the text into a displayed equation.
Add a footnote to the first sentence of the section.
Add a second \cite to an existing references.bib (add a new @article entry too) and cite it in the text.

extra_files.content: @article{doe2019, title={A Study}, author={Doe, Jane}, year={2019}, journal={J. Things}}

instruction: Add an enumerate list of two steps to the methods section.
Add a \subsection inside the existing section with a sentence.
Fix the broken document: there is a missing \end{document}. Add it.
Add the graphicx package to the preamble (do not add an actual image).
Add a centered displayed equation for the sum of the first n integers (load amsmath).
Create a new appendix.tex with an \section*{Appendix} and \input it from main.tex.
